areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper

leaves.label: The All-CNN-C model is implemented exactly as in Table 2 (Section 3.1): every max-pooling layer of base model C is replaced by a 3x3 convolu
leaves.why: code/models.py:_cifar_stage (letter='C', variant='allcnn') replaces each pool with nn.Conv2d(out_ch, out_ch, 3, padding=1, stride=2) (out channels == in channels), and CIFARNet's head is conv3x3-192 → conv1x1-192 → conv1x1-num_classes → spatial mean (forward: x.mean(dim=[2,3])) w

leaves.label: The three base models A, B, and C from Table 1 (Section 3.1) are implemented: model B as the Network-in-Network variant with one 1-by-1 conv
leaves.why: code/models.py implements A (single 5x5 conv per stage + maxpool), B (5x5 conv followed by NiN 1x1 conv + maxpool), and C (two 3x3 convs + maxpool), all topped by the conv3x3-192/conv1x1-192/conv1x1-10 + GAP head, and outputs/* contain a_base, b_base, c_base cell results.

leaves.label: The Strided-CNN-C variant (Table 2, Section 3.1) is implemented by removing max-pooling and increasing the stride of the convolution layer p
leaves.why: code/models.py:_cifar_stage variant='strided' for letter C removes MaxPool and makes the second 3x3 conv stride=2 (stride increased from 1 to 2, covering the same region), with c_strided_cifar10_noaug cells present in outputs/ and a_strided measured in metrics.json (test_error_pc

leaves.label: The ConvPool-CNN-C variant (Table 2, Section 3.1) is implemented by placing a dense convolution layer, with the same kernel size as the resp
leaves.why: code/models.py:_cifar_stage variant='convpool' inserts an extra dense nn.Conv2d(out_ch, out_ch, 3, padding=1)+ReLU immediately before nn.MaxPool2d(3, stride=2) — a 3x3 conv matching the 3x3 pooling kernel — and c_convpool_cifar10_noaug cells exist in outputs/.

leaves.label: Guided backpropagation is implemented as defined in Section 4 / Figure 1: when backpropagating through a ReLU, values are masked to zero whe
leaves.why: code/guided_backprop.py:GuidedBackpropReLU.backward_hook computes guided_grad = grad * (grad > 0).float() * act_mask with act_mask = (output > 0).float(), zeroing where either the incoming gradient or forward ReLU output is negative, uses no pooling switches, and produced artifac

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 (Krizhevsky & Hinton, 2009) images are whitened and contrast normalized following Goodfellow et al. (2013), as stated
leaves.why: code/data.py implements per-sample GCN (_gcn) plus ZCA whitening fitted on training data with eps=0.1 per Goodfellow et al. 2013 (compute_zca_matrix, ZCAPreprocessor) as the default path (load_cifar use_zca=True) for both cifar10 and cifar100, with the augment-raw→GCN→ZCA orderin

leaves.label: The data-augmentation runs use exactly the augmentations of Section 3.2: horizontally flipped copies of all images plus random translations
leaves.why: code/data.py:_augment implements only hflip plus random translation bounded by pad=5 (max ±5 px in each dimension) and nothing else, but uses online random flipping rather than adding flipped copies of all images as Section 3.2 describes.

leaves.label: The CIFAR-100 experiment reuses the identical All-CNN-C model and identical data pipeline and hyperparameters (learning rate and its schedul
leaves.why: outputs/.../c_allcnn_cifar100_aug/ exists using the same CIFARNet('C','allcnn') code path and the same data.py pipeline (load_cifar handles cifar100 identically with GCN+ZCA), but the per-cell config showing identical lr/schedule is not in the provided evidence.

leaves.label: Training logs/configs show all CIFAR networks trained with stochastic gradient descent with fixed momentum 0.9 for a total of 350 epochs, wi
leaves.why: metrics.json shows epochs_total=350 and epochs_run=350 for a_base/a_strided, and the measured train_loss_history/test_acc_history show clear step drops at epoch ~200 (0.674→0.558) and ~250 (0.520→0.498) consistent with the S=[200,250,300] ×0.1 schedule, but the momentum setting i

leaves.label: Run artifacts show the learning rate γ was searched per model over exactly the fixed set {0.25, 0.1, 0.05, 0.01}, with results reported for
leaves.why: metrics.json records 'lr': 0.05 and 'best_lr': 0.05 (a value from the paper's set, with a best_lr field implying selection), but no run artifacts evidence the actual four-value search per model.

leaves.label: Configs/code apply dropout with probability 20% on the input image and 50% after each pooling layer (or after the layer replacing pooling),
leaves.why: code/models.py:CIFARNet applies nn.Dropout(0.2) on the input and nn.Dropout(0.5) after each stage's pooling layer or its replacement exactly as Section 3.2 specifies, but no provided file evidences λ=0.001 weight decay.

leaves.label: The ImageNet run trains the upscaled All-CNN-B with 12 convolutional layers (conv1–conv12, Table 6 in the Appendix) for 450,000 iterations w
leaves.why: Only an untrained ImageNetAllCNNB class exists in code/models.py with a docstring of the schedule; no 450k-iteration training, batch-64 config, or measured ImageNet metric is anywhere in the evidence.

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 results are reported as test error (%) on the standard held-out test sets, separately for the no-augmentation and aug
leaves.why: metrics.json reports test_error_pct (15.61 for a_base, 19.49 for a_strided) on the held-out CIFAR-10 test set with augment=false flags and a per_dataset table, and outputs/ contains separate *_aug and *_noaug cells including c_allcnn_cifar100_aug, but the consolidated cross-setti

leaves.label: ILSVRC-2012 performance is measured as Top-1 validation error evaluated only on the center 224x224 patch of each validation image, matching
leaves.why: The only mention of center-224 evaluation is a docstring in models.py:ImageNetAllCNNB; no center-crop evaluation pipeline or measured Top-1 validation error exists in the evidence.

leaves.label: Class probabilities at evaluation are produced by the paper's fully convolutional inference path (Section 2): the topmost 1-by-1 convolution
leaves.why: code/models.py:CIFARNet.forward produces per-position class scores from the final nn.Conv2d(192, num_classes, 1) and averages over all spatial positions via x.mean(dim=[2,3]) before the softmax/cross-entropy, with no fully connected layer anywhere in the model, and metrics.json t

areas.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaves.label: Reproduced base model A achieves approximately 12.5% test error on CIFAR-10 without data augmentation, matching the result reported in Secti
leaves.why: metrics.json per_model.a_base.cifar10_noaug records test_error_pct=15.61 (test_accuracy=0.8439) over 350 epochs with best_lr=0.05 — a genuinely trained base Model A in the right regime, but not within the ±1% band of 12.5%.

leaves.label: The reproduced metrics reproduce the qualitative ordering of Table 3 (Section 3.2): Strided-CNN-C performs worse than base model C, while Al
leaves.why: Output cells for all C variants exist with metrics.json files (outputs/.../c_base_cifar10_noaug, c_strided_cifar10_noaug, c_convpool_cifar10_noaug, c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug) and the analogous measured A-family ordering holds (a_strided 19.49% > a_base 15.61% error), but the C-model

leaves.label: Reproduced All-CNN-C test error on CIFAR-10 (with and/or without augmentation) is at or below the corresponding base-model-C error, reproduc
leaves.why: The runs were executed — outputs/.../c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug, c_allcnn_cifar10_aug and c_base_cifar10_noaug each contain metrics.json/training_curves.json per the file listing — but the measured error values required to verify the paper's central All-CNN-C vs base-C claim are not

leaves.label: The reproduced ImageNet All-CNN-B achieves a Top-1 validation error close to 41.2% on the ILSVRC-2012 center 224x224 evaluation, with a mode
leaves.why: models.py defines ImageNetAllCNNB but marks ImageNet out of scope, and no measured Top-1 ILSVRC result exists in metrics.json or any output directory.

leaves.label: The repository contains runnable source code and configs for every evaluated variant — base models A/B/C, Strided-CNN-A/B/C, ConvPool-CNN-A/
leaves.why: code/models.py (CIFARNet + MODEL_REGISTRY) implements all 12 variants (A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn) with input Dropout(0.2) and Dropout(0.5) after each pooling stage, and the measured a_base run (epochs_total=350, train_loss_history step-drops at epochs ~200 and ~250) ev

leaves.label: Per-run training logs and a machine-readable metrics file (e.g. metrics.json) record the final test error for each model variant on CIFAR-10
leaves.why: The outputs listing shows a per-cell <cell>.log, metrics.json, training_curves.json and cell_manifest.json for every variant cell (all 12 CIFAR-10 cells plus c_allcnn_cifar10_aug and c_allcnn_cifar100_aug), and the visible record schema (per_model.a_base: test_error_pct, lr, best

leaves.label: Saved visualization artifacts exist for the guided-backpropagation method of Section 4: reconstructed feature visualizations for lower layer
leaves.why: outputs/.../c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/fig_relu_masking.png and guided_backprop.json exist, generated by code/guided_backprop.py whose GuidedBackpropReLU implements the Section 4 rule guided_grad = grad * (grad>0) * act_mask on the trained All-CNN-C, covering the higher-layer ('and/o

leaf_scores.justification: metrics.json per_model.a_base.cifar10_noaug records test_error_pct=15.61 (test_accuracy=0.8439) over 350 epochs with best_lr=0.05 — a genuinely trained base Model A in the right regime, but not within the ±1% band of 12.5%.

Output cells for all C variants exist with metrics.json files (outputs/.../c_base_cifar10_noaug, c_strided_cifar10_noaug, c_convpool_cifar10_noaug, c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug) and the analogous measured A-family ordering holds (a_strided 19.49% > a_base 15.61% error), but the C-model values needed to confirm the claimed ordering are not readable in the evidence.

The runs were executed — outputs/.../c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug, c_allcnn_cifar10_aug and c_base_cifar10_noaug each contain metrics.json/training_curves.json per the file listing — but the measured error values required to verify the paper's central All-CNN-C vs base-C claim are not readable in the supplied metrics.json.
models.py defines ImageNetAllCNNB but marks ImageNet out of scope, and no measured Top-1 ILSVRC result exists in metrics.json or any output directory.

code/models.py (CIFARNet + MODEL_REGISTRY) implements all 12 variants (A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn) with input Dropout(0.2) and Dropout(0.5) after each pooling stage, and the measured a_base run (epochs_total=350, train_loss_history step-drops at epochs ~200 and ~250) evidences the [200,250,300] schedule, but momentum/weight-decay values cannot be confirmed from the evidence.

The outputs listing shows a per-cell <cell>.log, metrics.json, training_curves.json and cell_manifest.json for every variant cell (all 12 CIFAR-10 cells plus c_allcnn_cifar10_aug and c_allcnn_cifar100_aug), and the visible record schema (per_model.a_base: test_error_pct, lr, best_lr, seed, cell_id, epochs_run) is traceable to the producing config/learning rate.

outputs/.../c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/fig_relu_masking.png and guided_backprop.json exist, generated by code/guided_backprop.py whose GuidedBackpropReLU implements the Section 4 rule guided_grad = grad * (grad>0) * act_mask on the trained All-CNN-C, covering the higher-layer ('and/or') case but not the conv1–conv3 visualizations.

code/models.py:_cifar_stage (letter='C', variant='allcnn') replaces each pool with nn.Conv2d(out_ch, out_ch, 3, padding=1, stride=2) (out channels == in channels), and CIFARNet's head is conv3x3-192 → conv1x1-192 → conv1x1-num_classes → spatial mean (forward: x.mean(dim=[2,3])) with only conv+ReLU layers, matching Table 2; the c_allcnn_* output cells confirm it ran.

code/models.py implements A (single 5x5 conv per stage + maxpool), B (5x5 conv followed by NiN 1x1 conv + maxpool), and C (two 3x3 convs + maxpool), all topped by the conv3x3-192/conv1x1-192/conv1x1-10 + GAP head, and outputs/* contain a_base, b_base, c_base cell results.

code/models.py:_cifar_stage variant='strided' for letter C removes MaxPool and makes the second 3x3 conv stride=2 (stride increased from 1 to 2, covering the same region), with c_strided_cifar10_noaug cells present in outputs/ and a_strided measured in metrics.json (test_error_pct=19.49).

code/models.py:_cifar_stage variant='convpool' inserts an extra dense nn.Conv2d(out_ch, out_ch, 3, padding=1)+ReLU immediately before nn.MaxPool2d(3, stride=2) — a 3x3 conv matching the 3x3 pooling kernel — and c_convpool_cifar10_noaug cells exist in outputs/.

code/guided_backprop.py:GuidedBackpropReLU.backward_hook computes guided_grad = grad * (grad > 0).float() * act_mask with act_mask = (output > 0).float(), zeroing where either the incoming gradient or forward ReLU output is negative, uses no pooling switches, and produced artifacts (outputs/.../c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/guided_backprop.json, fig_relu_masking.png).

code/data.py implements per-sample GCN (_gcn) plus ZCA whitening fitted on training data with eps=0.1 per Goodfellow et al. 2013 (compute_zca_matrix, ZCAPreprocessor) as the default path (load_cifar use_zca=True) for both cifar10 and cifar100, with the augment-raw→GCN→ZCA ordering handled in CIFARDataset.__getitem__.

code/data.py:_augment implements only hflip plus random translation bounded by pad=5 (max ±5 px in each dimension) and nothing else, but uses online random flipping rather than adding flipped copies of all images as Section 3.2 describes.

outputs/.../c_allcnn_cifar100_aug/ exists using the same CIFARNet('C','allcnn') code path and the same data.py pipeline (load_cifar handles cifar100 identically with GCN+ZCA), but the per-cell config showing identical lr/schedule is not in the provided evidence.

metrics.json shows epochs_total=350 and epochs_run=350 for a_base/a_strided, and the measured train_loss_history/test_acc_history show clear step drops at epoch ~200 (0.674→0.558) and ~250 (0.520→0.498) consistent with the S=[200,250,300] ×0.1 schedule, but the momentum setting is not visible in any provided file.
metrics.json records 'lr': 0.05 and 'best_lr': 0.05 (a value from the paper's set, with a best_lr field implying selection), but no run artifacts evidence the actual four-value search per model.

code/models.py:CIFARNet applies nn.Dropout(0.2) on the input and nn.Dropout(0.5) after each stage's pooling layer or its replacement exactly as Section 3.2 specifies, but no provided file evidences λ=0.001 weight decay.

Only an untrained ImageNetAllCNNB class exists in code/models.py with a docstring of the schedule; no 450k-iteration training, batch-64 config, or measured ImageNet metric is anywhere in the evidence.

metrics.json reports test_error_pct (15.61 for a_base, 19.49 for a_strided) on the held-out CIFAR-10 test set with augment=false flags and a per_dataset table, and outputs/ contains separate *_aug and *_noaug cells including c_allcnn_cifar100_aug, but the consolidated cross-setting report is incomplete.
The only mention of center-224 evaluation is a docstring in models.py:ImageNetAllCNNB; no center-crop evaluation pipeline or measured Top-1 validation error exists in the evidence.

code/models.py:CIFARNet.forward produces per-position class scores from the final nn.Conv2d(192, num_classes, 1) and averages over all spatial positions via x.mean(dim=[2,3]) before the softmax/cross-entropy, with no fully connected layer anywhere in the model, and metrics.json test_accuracy values (0.8439) were measured through this path.

weak_leaves.justification: models.py defines ImageNetAllCNNB but marks ImageNet out of scope, and no measured Top-1 ILSVRC result exists in metrics.json or any output directory.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: Only an untrained ImageNetAllCNNB class exists in code/models.py with a docstring of the schedule; no 450k-iteration training, batch-64 config, or measured ImageNet metric is anywhere in the evidence.
The only mention of center-224 evaluation is a docstring in models.py:ImageNetAllCNNB; no center-crop evaluation pipeline or measured Top-1 validation error exists in the evidence.

Output cells for all C variants exist with metrics.json files (outputs/.../c_base_cifar10_noaug, c_strided_cifar10_noaug, c_convpool_cifar10_noaug, c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug) and the analogous measured A-family ordering holds (a_strided 19.49% > a_base 15.61% error), but the C-model values needed to confirm the claimed ordering are not readable in the evidence.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: The runs were executed — outputs/.../c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug, c_allcnn_cifar10_aug and c_base_cifar10_noaug each contain metrics.json/training_curves.json per the file listing — but the measured error values required to verify the paper's central All-CNN-C vs base-C claim are not readable in the supplied metrics.json.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: metrics.json records 'lr': 0.05 and 'best_lr': 0.05 (a value from the paper's set, with a best_lr field implying selection), but no run artifacts evidence the actual four-value search per model.

metrics.json per_model.a_base.cifar10_noaug records test_error_pct=15.61 (test_accuracy=0.8439) over 350 epochs with best_lr=0.05 — a genuinely trained base Model A in the right regime, but not within the ±1% band of 12.5%.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: code/models.py (CIFARNet + MODEL_REGISTRY) implements all 12 variants (A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn) with input Dropout(0.2) and Dropout(0.5) after each pooling stage, and the measured a_base run (epochs_total=350, train_loss_history step-drops at epochs ~200 and ~250) evidences the [200,250,300] schedule, but momentum/weight-decay values cannot be confirmed from the evidence.
